theScene: You make your way to the ghostly sisters' abode, which is a bit of a ways away from the village proper, past a copse of trees and down a winding path, most likely the better to contribute to the aura of spookiness that the riddling trio is fond of cultivating... with mixed results.

At any rate, their dwelling certainly sticks out quite starkly amid Sleim Vllge's cheerfully pastoral, rustic landscape: a skull-shaped house whose maw gapes forebodingly, a set of double doors set into its mouth as if ready to be crushed between its teeth... Though you also can't help but find the proportions rather cute, the skeletal head looking to be grinning invitingly rather than snarling.

Grabbing the massive handles, you pull at the stately gates which creak and groan spectacularly as they swing open with deliberately manufactured ponderousness, leaving the way open for you to slip within the darkened interior.

From the door, the entrance leads straight into an earthy expanse dug directly into a hillside, the interior looking quite like the Caverns, right down to glowing crystals probably harvested from there, albeit with more solid stonework and a variety of furniture for the slime ghosts to feel at home, including quite gothic-looking floor and wall coverings, a crystal-bearing chandelier, and even a large four-poster bed for them to sleep in.

However, you barely have the time to take in the decor and let your eyes accustom themselves to the otherworldly underground luminosity before the three ghosts spring out of seemingly nowhere right in front of you, dramatically waving their arms and letting out a chorus of ghastly wails!
What brings you to our humble abooOOooOde?
Mycia's cheerful greeting and welcoming wave immediately break the mood the ghostly slimes were going for, leaving Virosa sighing despondently while Amanita sputters.
D-Don't be so casual! We're supposed to spook him!
That's not the point! You- Ah, whatever... What's up, [ThePlayerName]?

Making peace with the fact that there won't be any spooking happening, the grumpy-looking monster girl beckons you inside, the three sisters letting you settle in then watching you curiously, eager to know the purpose of your visit.

You seem to be catching the trio at a busy time, Amanita holding a pail full of compost which Mycia carefully lays over a large dirt bed covered with mushrooms, while Virosa is lounging in a corner with an air of intense concentration on her face, a pencil in her hand and a sheet of paper in front of her, most likely working on a riddle.

Nonetheless, they pause their work as you step into their home, discarding their serious focused expressions for welcoming smiles, Amanita and Virosa respectively putting down her bucket and pencil while Mycia gives one of the mushrooms a gentle caring pat before turning to you.
Hiya [ThePlayerName]! What can we do ya for?

And as you step within, you see three familiar shapes stir within the confines of the aforementioned bed, the sisters looking like they were in the middle of sleeping and have been awakened by your entrance, the trio letting out a mixture of mumbles, groans, and yawns as they blearily take you in.
However, if they mind the intrusion, they do not show it, instead smiling and waving welcomingly at you from the covers.
Greetings, [ThePlayerName], and pray forgive our rather disheveled state... What brings you here?

But before you can even step in, you're caught by surprise by a blue slime suddenly careening out of the cavern, the monster girl laughing madly as she's getting chased by the three mistresses of the house who wail, flail, and grimace fearsomely!
Not so faaaAaaaAst! You fooOoOOlish intruder, you came here to your doOoOoOm!
You will be forever cursed! CuuUuUrsed!

With all the speed that her gooey body allows her, the slime zips past you and runs down the path back to the village, giggling all the way under the satisfied gazes of her 'tormentors' who notice you with happy smiles.
Oh, hey, [ThePlayerName]! Did you come for a visit?
We're just done dealing with a pesky interloper, as you can see.
Which leaves us free to host you. Do come in!
Still exchanging satisfied grins at a job well done, the trio hover back into their domain, beckoning you in.
Waving you off, the ghostly trio close the door behind you, producing a dramatic sepulchral slam and leaving you back into Sleim Vllge after a short walk back to the main square.
Sure! We're always up for a chinwag!
What would you like to talk about?
... Did they phase through the bed?
Anything else you wanted to see us about?

Excitedly grabbing your hand, the eager ghost takes you further into her home, where you can see that a sizable portion of the cavern has been dedicated to mushroom-growing, rows upon rows of dark soil housing countless fungi of all shapes and sizes, filling the area with a strong, downright heady earthy smell which yet doesn't seem to inconvenience their growers, Mycia enthusiastically waving at the fruits of their labor.

I have to admit, I didn't expect our crops to be this abundant... Not that I was doubting our skills, but Sleim Vllge's lands are so fertile it's almost scary. Good thing our neighbors aren't shy about helping, or we'd be spending our days up to our elbows in mushrooms.

Having not let go of your hand, Mycia pulls you up to another spot where the ground has been dug into a shallow pool filled with wet, thick compost forming a sort of artificial bog where thick, fuzzy-looking bushes are growing so tightly that it's hard to tell them apart, each bristling with bunches of white, puffy... {i}marshmallows{/i}.

Even with the small sugary treats plainly growing on branches right in front of you, it's hard to believe that this is actually how marshmallows are made... Perhaps a peculiarity of Lucidia? At any rate, Mycia seems oblivious to your puzzlement, happily plucking one of the plump little fruits which she hands you.
Here, have a taste! They're best fresh off the vine.♪
And they are nicely contributing to Sleim Vllge's growth, too. They may not be as abundant as mushrooms, but they are something of an exotic rarity, and fetch quite the tidy price as a result.~

While listening to the ghostly slimes, you take a tentative nibble of your gift, and cannot but admit that it {i}does{/i} taste like a marshmallow... Except maybe fuller, richer, and juicier? It's a strange feeling, but not an unpleasant one, which leaves you with no reluctance to toss the rest of it into your mouth and chew with relish as you are walked back to the house's entrance after that brief visit.
As you can see, true to our word, we have been doing our part. Who would have thought that something we initially picked up as more of a hobby would end up contributing to a town's prosperity?
Which, I gotta admit, is a pretty good feeling.
The three of them exchange nods of agreement while you swallow your marshmallow, then they turn back to you in expectant unison, waiting to see what else you want to talk about.
Hm, what's there to say... We've lived there for a while... A lot of ghost slimes do, since it's a nice environment for us: cool, dark, and quiet.

Not to mention full of interesting stuff and places! Lots of nooks and crannies where you can dig for rare plantlife, precious minerals, and old trinkets... Or where you can hide to set up a spoooOoOoky surprise!
While the excitable Mycia floats to and fro, making dramatic 'OooOOoOO' sounds, Virosa chuckles affectionately and carries on.

It is indeed a place with an undeniable charm of its own, especially if you have a penchant for geology. The crystals in particular are uniquely beautiful, and in that regard, I cannot but admit that our hosts have done a {i}splendid{/i} job exporting them to build and furnish this place.
She looks around the becrystaled gothic grotto with a deeply pleased gleam in her eyes, though her expression unexpectedly sours with distasteful recollection as she adds.

But back to the Caverns, despite their appeals, they are certainly lacking in... ah, {i}amenities{/i}. To my own chagrin and shame, I must confess that we ourselves lived in what was more akin to a base camp than to a bona fide dwelling. In that respect, we {i}undoubtedly{/i} came out better off with our current accommodations, to say nothing of our neighbors, who have proven much more sociable and amenable than salarisi.

Or adventurers! A lot of them pass through the Caverns, and one and all, they're a bunch of lewdies! Whenever you cross paths with one, they're sure to pounce you without even a 'Hello!' or a 'Wanna tussle?'!
W-Well, sure, but i-it's not the same! We did it for, like, {i}fun{/i}!
And still do.~ We may have a home of our own now, but a brisk walk through the Caverns still makes for quite the pleasant and {i}invigorating{/i} experience, he he...

While Virosa chuckles salaciously at her not-so-subtle innuendo, the other two give her a deadpan stare, though they don't contradict her either, hinting that they probably partake of the same guilty pleasure...
Speaking of the Caverns' 'fauna', though, I'll miss the matangos a bit. They had interesting stories to tell.
I don't know... They're kinda mean and nasty, and they like scaring people... That should be {i}our{/i} thing!

Mycia sulks emphatically at the memory, prompting her two sisters to comfort her dotingly, the ghostly monster girl getting treated to a round of hugs and pats while the trio await your next query, having apparently said all they had to say on the subject.
Oh, interested in ghost slimes, huh?

Hehe, we're made of the condensed feelings of the restless dead: rage, lust, despair, greed, hunger... All amalgamated into the phantasmal bodies you see before you! One could even say that the very souuUuUls of the departed live on within us!
The three of them hover around you in a circle as Amanita speaks, giggling and snickering eerily, until Virosa drops the act with a sigh.

... At least, that is the myth, but in truth, while our bodies are more diaphanous, act as greater mana conductors, and we enjoy dark places more than the average slime, we are fundamentally not so different. Just like most other of our gooey kind, we are impervious to most physical harm, are keenly sensitive to magic, and reproduce via mitosis.
That's actually how we're 'sisters'! Virosa and I both are from Amanita!
Hm? No we aren't? Not to contradict, Mycie, but we actually both split off from Virosa.
I-I thought you and I were both from Mycie, though!?

The other two slimes sputter at the notion, the three sisters exchanging baffled looks as they try and fail to make sense of this mystery, cueing you to carry on with a different topic before they develop an existential crisis...
The sistren break into happy smiles at the notion, excitedly bobbing in place as they cheerfully respond.
It started out as a way to stave off boredom, but we got more and more into it, and before we realized it, it kinda became our thing.
And a great 'thing' it is! A splendid way to keep one's mind sharp, and also to confuse and confound visitors, hehehe.~
We've actually come up with a new one, too! Wanna hear it?

Your refusal breaks the trio's stride, the ghosts sporting shocked expressions as if you'd just committed a glaring faux pas, Amanita being the first to make a stammering recovery from the seemingly outrageous rebuke.
Despite her answer, an undeniable air of gloom has settled over your now silent hosts. You should probably hurry up and find another topic to bring up...
After another round of hyped giggles, the white slimes look at each other and exchange nods, all three of them lining up showily in front of you and intoning with dramatic coordination.
Time seems to freeze as they remain silent after their synchronized declamation, eagerly awaiting your answer.
The riddlers exchange somewhat puzzled glances at your apparently unexpected answer, conferring silently until Amanita eventually volunteers.
Well, that's not really what we had in mind...

But that doesn't really sound {i}wrong{/i} either? After all, everyone knows the Black Knight, but not many know what she looks like under that armor of hers... And I don't know {i}anyone{/i} who'd mess with her.
A spell of somewhat awkward silence ensues, which Virosa breaks with customary good-humored grace.

Well, it looks like we will need to give that one a bit more time in the oven, for the sake of making it a little bit less ambiguous... For now, we'll consider it {b}correct{/b} and give you this for humoring us.

Smoothly, she produces a small waxed paper bag filled with an assortment of cutesy multicolored marshmallows, which you gratefully accept and pocket. While such a snack looks like it would be of questionable usefulness in the middle of combat, it should make for an enjoyable treat, if nothing else.
Yeah, we'll definitely need to think more on it...
In the meantime, anything else you wanted to talk about?

Merrily, Mycia and Virosa spin around you, letting out whoops and cheers for your success. They definitely feel more relaxed and lively about it than they were back when you first encountered them in the Caverns and went through their gauntlet of riddles, probably owing to the stakes being lower and chiller now.

With playful gravitas, she presents you with a small waxed paper bag filled with an assortment of cutesy multicolored marshmallows, which you gratefully accept and pocket. While such a snack looks like it would be of questionable usefulness in the middle of combat, it should make for an enjoyable treat, if nothing else.
Glad that this one worked out well! We'll need to think of more.♪
But for now, let's get back to the chatting. Is there anything else you wanted to talk to us about, [ThePlayerName]?
While Mycia giggles with easygoing gaiety and Virosa smiles benevolently, Amanita doesn't share their indulgence, pouting scoldingly at your response.
I don't know if you're trying to be cute or just want to have a lark, but that's definitely off the mark and {b}incorrect{/b}.
Aw, don't be a spoilsport, Nita! There's nothing wrong with [ThePlayerName] having faith in Perp!
The cheerful ghost embraces her sister and musses her hair to her undaunted grumbling discontent, leaving Virosa to chime in.
Regretfully, though, I indeed cannot say that the description we provided would apply to our dear Perpetua, no matter what heights of esteem we have towards her and her enterprising spirit.
... But who knows, maybe in a few years, if she keeps progressing with as much gusto, she might attain such fame and authority?
As the suave ghost trills mysteriously, her moody peer concludes while still in the middle of getting hugged by Mycia.
For now, though, no dice... And no, no retry this time.
Nothing to do but move on to the next conversation topic, then, it seems...
The ghosts snicker and giggle mischievously at your answer.
Sorry, [ThePlayerName], but I don't think you command {i}that much{/i} respect.

Yes, unless we are sorely mistaken, I don't believe that none would dare ignore or deny you... But should your mettle be much greater than we figure, you are {i}very welcome{/i} to show us proof of your greatness.~
While Virosa rather unsubtly makes eyes at you and glances towards the room's big canopy bed, Mycia gives a little blushing chuckle whereas Amanita groans and rolls her eyes.
You couldn't not be horny for more than ten minutes, huh?
Oh, come now, don't tell me you don't share the sentiment.~
But back to the riddle, that's unfortunately {b}incorrect{/b}. Looks like we'll have to come up with a better riddle... Or like you'll have to come up with less jokey answers.

She leaves it at that while Virosa continues to watch you lasciviously and Mycia eyes you with similar interest, the ghosts nonetheless not pushing the topic further and leaving you to decide what to carry on with.
Before Virosa can finish the word, her two sisters hurriedly pounce her, Amanita covering her mouth to stop her from talking while Mycia sputters.
N-No, that's not right! We {i}definitely{/i} agreed that the answer was the Demon Queen!
I swear, Rosa, you and your sappy inclinations...

Despite her fellow riddlers' vigorous disapproval and remonstrations, it takes a few more moments of tussling with Virosa to bring her into line, the romance-loving ghost eventually relenting with poor grace.
Hrmmgrg... Alright, that's {b}incorrect{/b}... {size=-5}But I swear I will have my revenge through another riddle of my own.{/size}
Paying no mind to her grudgeful grumbling, Mycia and Amanita turn back to you with sighs of relief, smiling brightly as if nothing happened.
Ooooh, {i}someone{/i} got hooked on riddles, huh?

Unfortunately, I'm sorry to say that we haven't come up with a proper new one yet, so we'll have to put a pin in that... We've been pretty busy lately with all the mushroom-growing and Sleim Vllge activities and stuff, which hasn't left a lot of time for riddle-making.
... But yeah, no other riddles for you for the time being, sorry.
Amanita scoffs at the barely disguised lustful comment, but conspicuously doesn't contradict her sister either, instead commenting.
You could say that she hasn't been very sociable, what with her not leaving that orchard of hers much, but whenever we visit, she's always kind towards us... Assuming she isn't just sleeping.

She's a real whizz, too! She's not the type to boast, but she's actually been able to work her magic on our mushrooms and make jellies out of them that make you {i}super smart{/i}! Silver's selling them now. You should definitely give her wares a look if you haven't already.♪

The other two ghosts nod affirmingly at the recommendation, all three of them seeming to share an undisguised respect for the green slime and her abilities, Mycia pausing at the thought and adding conversationally.
You know what? Now that I think about it, she reminds me a bit of Nova.
Nova? ... Oh, yeah! Yeah, I can definitely see the resemblance.

Virosa wordlessly reacts to the comparison with a lascivious, vacuous giggle, her mind probably lost in fantasies of lewd baths with Ambrosia and Nova, her unabashed lecherousness making even Mycia groan as she and Amanita turn to you almost pleadingly, having apparently said all they had to say about their green neighbor and mutely asking you to move on to another subject before their sister gets more graphic with her ideas.

Indeed~ We've got a wonderful place to live in, charming neighbors, a peaceful life... What's not to love? I even can't but admit that the whole 'Sleim Vllge Renovation' business has proven more engaging than I'd have expected. Who would have thought that a trio of free spirits like us would get roped into such a project, and enjoy it too?~
Mycia and Amanita nod approvingly to her words, though an unexpected shadow then falls on the latter's face.
Th-Though, if I had to mention {i}one{/i} issue, it's that it's still scary to live with the open sky being, like, {i}right outside our door{/i}...
Ugh- Yeah! Good thing it's easy to find someone who's willing to act as a tether whenever we need to go out.

While Amanita opines seriously at that, you easily notice Virosa giving a half-weary, half-easygoing smirk at her sisters' antics... You also can easily picture the scaredy ghosts fearfully going around the town staunchly holding hands with helpful blue slimes, the idea certainly painting quite the quaint scene...
But that aside, it's all been swell! We're grateful to you and Perpetua for making it happen, and we've been doing our best to give back!
The subject ends on that positive note, the ghosts having nothing more of substance to add, prompting you to move on with the conversation.

EventText.NameOfScene: ... Did they phase through the bed?
theScene: You bring up something you noticed during your lewd romp with the sisters, and which has been on your mind since: how could they pass through their bed when ghost slimes are supposedly unable to phase through inorganic materials? At least, according to your Grimoire's Encyclopedia...

Your question elicits various reactions of surprise and shock from the trio, and for a moment, you actually wonder if they somehow didn't realize it themselves up to now, until Amanita responds with a guilty wince.
Oh, right, we never told you...
... Even though it's {i}so cool{/i}! Come look!
Followed by her sisters and yourself, Mycia excitedly floats up to the aforementioned four-poster bed, where she demonstratively grabs the thick quilt covering the opulent piece of furniture.

Suddenly, her fingers phase through the fabric in her grasp, which drops back with a soft muffled sound while the ghost slime emphatically shows off her palms to you, as if she were a magician doing a 'nothing in my hands' demonstration after a trick.
While this does confirm your assumption, you can't help but note that it doesn't really explain anything, though Virosa seems to realize so as well, chiming in helpfully.

At the risk of breaking the magic, though, I must mention that neither Mycie nor the rest of us three have done anything special... Ghost slimes {i}are{/i} incapable of passing through solid matter, and we are no exception in that regard. The 'trick' lies in the bed itself.
Yeah... I couldn't tell you how it works {i}exactly{/i}, but the entire bed is infused with ectoplasm, which means that ghost slimes like us can either touch it or pass through it as we want.
Since we're talking about it, though, where does it even come from? Feels like something like that would be pretty rare... A-And {i}pricy{/i}.

While Amanita just gives a 'Beats me.' shrug in response, Virosa puts a finger to her lips with a thoughtful hum, making sure that her conspicuous gesture has drawn everyone's attention before saying.

Wellll... I actually broached the topic with Silver, and she told me that she got it from some solitary alchemist who crafted it as an experiment, but then didn't have a use for it... So she was able to procure it for rather cheap. 'Verygooddealhehehe.' were her words.
If anything, this revelation seems to make Mycia and Amanita all the more perplexed, the former bringing up what appears to be on everyone's mind.

... Is that actually something that happens? N-Not that I'd think Silver would lie, but I didn't think she had those kinds of connections? I-Is there some sort of bedmancer on Lucidia that I'm not aware of?
A silence follows this question, which none of the sisters seem to be able to answer this time, Amanita eventually shrugging again and concluding.
Well... That went off on a bit of a tangent, but anyway, there you have it: that's how the bed works.
Oh my, raring to go again already? ... Well, so am I.♥︎
Finally! I was wondering how long you were going to make us wait for.

Virosa's forward lecherousness leaves Mycia and Amanita scoffing and groaning, but even they display a rather undisguised interest at the prospect, stealing glances at your body with an eager blush on their cheeks, fidgeting expectantly as they wait for you to suggest something.

The ghost slime seems positively outraged at your unexpected change of heart, her outburst leaving Amanita wincing and Mycia starting in shock as Virosa starts sputtering incoherently, looking like she's going to remain stuck in a loop of her own bafflement until her level-minded sister steps in.

Turning away with uncustomary petulance, the offended monster girl sulks in silence while Mycia does her best to comfort her and placate her bad mood, leaving Amanita to shrug and turn back to you, silently querying you for what you want to do instead.
The three ghost slimes exchange gleeful glances at your choice, looking back to you with a mixture of enthusiastically lascivious expressions.
Oh, you're gonna love this one!♪
Just make yourself comfortable and lie down there.

She points to the house's spacious four-poster bed, prompting you to walk up to it while discarding your clothes in the process, helped in that regard by the ectoplasmic trio who eagerly float up to you to assist you.

Virosa comments appreciatively as she pulls off your shirt while the other two take care of your lower garments, the trio unabashedly using the opportunity to trailingly run their hands over your frame with horny giggles and hums, their light, nimble, slightly cold touch sending enticing shivers through you and already starting to make your cock stiffen expectantly, the reaction being met with approving coos from your playful partners.

Under their teasing coaxing, you get on the bed and scoot over to the nest of pillows, the cover's silky smooth material making a pleasant soft rustling sound as your body rubs over it, the luxurious fabric gently caressing your skin on the way.

As you turn around to rest your head into the cushiony mass beneath you and let the yielding mattress embrace your back, you find yourself facing the three ghostly women who take you in with overt lecherousness, licking their lips and tittering pervertedly at the sight of you lying naked in their bed, offered to them.

Without a word, they descend together upon you, without hurry but with unhesitant decisiveness, Virosa delicately grabbing your face to pull you into a giggling kiss while Mycia takes care of your torso, letting out a hungry purr as she puts her head against your belly and lays her palms against your chest, laying a demonstrative smooch on your stomach.

... only to dive down straight into the bed, passing through the furniture as if it wasn't there, disappearing from your sight and leaving you alone with the remaining ghost sister who titters at your astonishment.
